content.problemStatement:
Chef went shopping and bought items worth XXX rupees (1≤X≤1001 \le X \le 1001≤X≤100). Unfortunately, Chef only has a single 100100100 rupees note.

Since Chef is weak at maths, can you help Chef in calculating what money he should get back after paying 100100100 rupees for those items?


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case consists of a single line containing an integer XXX, the total price of items Chef purchased.


Output Format
For each test case, output in a single line the money Chef has to receive back.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer X (1 ≤ X ≤ 100) – the total price of items Chef bought.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the amount of money Chef receives back after paying with a 100‑rupee note.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
For each test case we subtract the price from 100:
- X=1 → 100-1 = 99
- X=25 → 100-25 = 75
- X=100 → 100-100 = 0

solution.approach: For each test case the answer is simply the difference between the fixed note value (100) and the price X. Compute ans = 100 - X and print it. This is O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
